  • View page source

ictonyx.bootstrapÔÉÅ

Bootstrap confidence interval estimation.

Bootstrap confidence interval estimation for Ictonyx.

Provides non-parametric confidence intervals for effect sizes, mean differences, and arbitrary statistics. Designed for the small-sample regime (5-30 runs) typical of ML model comparison experiments.

Methods implemented:

  • Percentile: Simple quantile-based CI. Adequate for symmetric distributions.

  • BCa (Bias-Corrected and Accelerated): Gold-standard bootstrap CI. Corrects for both median bias and skewness in the bootstrap distribution. Recommended default for most use cases.

References

  • Efron, B. (1987). ‚ÄúBetter Bootstrap Confidence Intervals.‚Äù Journal of the American Statistical Association, 82(397), 171-185.

  • Efron, B. & Tibshirani, R. (1993). ‚ÄúAn Introduction to the Bootstrap.‚Äù Chapman & Hall.

  • DiCiccio, T.J. & Efron, B. (1996). ‚ÄúBootstrap Confidence Intervals.‚Äù Statistical Science, 11(3), 189-228.

class ictonyx.bootstrap.BootstrapCIResult(ci_lower, ci_upper, point_estimate, confidence_level, method, n_bootstrap, se_bootstrap, bootstrap_distribution=None)[source]ÔÉÅ

Bases: object

Result of a bootstrap confidence interval estimation.

Parameters:

  • ci_lower (float)

  • ci_upper (float)

  • point_estimate (float)

  • confidence_level (float)

  • method (str)

  • n_bootstrap (int)

  • se_bootstrap (float)

  • bootstrap_distribution (ndarray | None)

ci_lowerÔÉÅ

Lower bound of the confidence interval.

Type:

float

ci_upperÔÉÅ

Upper bound of the confidence interval.

Type:

float

point_estimateÔÉÅ

The statistic computed on the original (non-resampled) data.

Type:

float

confidence_levelÔÉÅ

The confidence level (e.g. 0.95 for a 95% CI).

Type:

float

methodÔÉÅ

The CI construction method (‚Äòpercentile‚Äô or ‚Äòbca‚Äô).

Type:

str

n_bootstrapÔÉÅ

Number of bootstrap resamples used.

Type:

int

se_bootstrapÔÉÅ

Standard error of the bootstrap distribution.

Type:

float

bootstrap_distributionÔÉÅ

The full array of bootstrap replications (available for plotting or diagnostics).

Type:

numpy.ndarray | None

ci_lower: floatÔÉÅ
ci_upper: floatÔÉÅ
point_estimate: floatÔÉÅ
confidence_level: floatÔÉÅ
method: strÔÉÅ
n_bootstrap: intÔÉÅ
se_bootstrap: floatÔÉÅ
bootstrap_distribution: ndarray | None = NoneÔÉÅ
ictonyx.bootstrap.bootstrap_ci(data, statistic_fn, n_bootstrap=10000, confidence=0.95, method='bca', random_state=None, return_distribution=False)[source]ÔÉÅ

Compute a bootstrap confidence interval for an arbitrary statistic.

This is the general-purpose engine. For common use cases, prefer the convenience functions bootstrap_mean_difference_ci and bootstrap_effect_size_ci, which handle two-sample data and call this function internally.

Parameters:

  • data (ndarray | Series | List[float]) ‚Äì 1-D array-like of observations.

  • statistic_fn (Callable[[ndarray], float]) ‚Äì A callable that takes a 1-D numpy array and returns a scalar float. This function is applied to each bootstrap resample.

  • n_bootstrap (int) ‚Äì Number of bootstrap resamples (default 10 000). Higher values give more precise CIs at the cost of compute time.

  • confidence (float) ‚Äì Confidence level in (0, 1). Default 0.95.

  • method (str) ‚Äì CI construction method. 'percentile' ‚Äî simple quantile method. 'bca' ‚Äî bias-corrected and accelerated (recommended).

  • random_state (int | Generator | None) ‚Äì Seed for reproducibility. Pass an integer for deterministic results.

  • return_distribution (bool) ‚Äì If True, the full bootstrap distribution is stored in the result (useful for plotting). Default False.

Returns:

BootstrapCIResult with CI bounds, point estimate, and diagnostics.

Raises:

ValueError ‚Äì If data has fewer than 2 observations, confidence is outside (0, 1), n_bootstrap < 100, or method is unrecognised.

Return type:

BootstrapCIResult

ictonyx.bootstrap.bootstrap_mean_difference_ci(group1, group2, n_bootstrap=10000, confidence=0.95, method='bca', random_state=None, return_distribution=False)[source]ÔÉÅ

Bootstrap CI for the difference in means (group1 - group2).

Resamples each group independently, computes the difference in means for each bootstrap replicate, and constructs a CI on that distribution.

This is the most directly actionable CI for model comparison: it tells you the plausible range of the true performance gap.

Parameters:

  • group1 (ndarray | Series | List[float]) ‚Äì Metric values for model 1 (e.g. accuracies from 10 runs).

  • group2 (ndarray | Series | List[float]) ‚Äì Metric values for model 2.

  • n_bootstrap (int) ‚Äì Number of bootstrap resamples (default 10 000).

  • confidence (float) ‚Äì Confidence level (default 0.95).

  • method (str) ‚Äì ‚Äòpercentile‚Äô or ‚Äòbca‚Äô (default ‚Äòbca‚Äô).

  • random_state (int | None) ‚Äì Seed for reproducibility.

  • return_distribution (bool) ‚Äì If True, store the full bootstrap distribution.

Returns:

BootstrapCIResult. The point_estimate is mean(group1) - mean(group2).

Return type:

BootstrapCIResult

ictonyx.bootstrap.bootstrap_effect_size_ci(group1, group2, n_bootstrap=10000, confidence=0.95, method='bca', pooled=True, random_state=None, return_distribution=False)[source]ÔÉÅ

Bootstrap CI for Cohen‚Äôs d effect size.

Computes Cohen‚Äôs d on each bootstrap resample and constructs a CI. This pairs naturally with the effect sizes already reported by compare_two_models ‚Äî it adds the uncertainty range around that point estimate.

Parameters:

  • group1 (ndarray | Series | List[float]) ‚Äì Metric values for model 1.

  • group2 (ndarray | Series | List[float]) ‚Äì Metric values for model 2.

  • n_bootstrap (int) ‚Äì Number of bootstrap resamples (default 10 000).

  • confidence (float) ‚Äì Confidence level (default 0.95).

  • method (str) ‚Äì ‚Äòpercentile‚Äô or ‚Äòbca‚Äô (default ‚Äòbca‚Äô).

  • pooled (bool) ‚Äì If True (default), use pooled standard deviation. If False, use group2‚Äôs std (Glass‚Äôs delta variant).

  • random_state (int | None) ‚Äì Seed for reproducibility.

  • return_distribution (bool) ‚Äì If True, store the full bootstrap distribution.

Returns:

BootstrapCIResult. The point_estimate is Cohen‚Äôs d on the original data.

Return type:

BootstrapCIResult

ictonyx.bootstrap.bootstrap_hedges_g_ci(group1, group2, n_bootstrap=10000, confidence=0.95, method='bca', random_state=None, return_distribution=False)[source]ÔÉÅ

Bootstrap CI for Hedges‚Äô g effect size.

Applies the Hedges‚Äô J correction on every bootstrap resample so that the CI and point estimate are for the same estimator. Use this instead of bootstrap_effect_size_ci() when the point estimate is Hedges‚Äô g (i.e., the Welch‚Äôs t-test path in compare_two_models()).

Parameters:

  • group1 (ndarray | Series | List[float]) ‚Äì Metric values for model 1.

  • group2 (ndarray | Series | List[float]) ‚Äì Metric values for model 2.

  • n_bootstrap (int) ‚Äì Number of bootstrap resamples. Default 10 000.

  • confidence (float) ‚Äì Confidence level. Default 0.95.

  • method (str) ‚Äì 'percentile' or 'bca'. Default 'bca'.

  • random_state (int | None) ‚Äì Seed for reproducibility.

  • return_distribution (bool) ‚Äì If True, store the full bootstrap distribution.

Returns:

BootstrapCIResult. point_estimate is Hedges‚Äô g computed on the original (non-resampled) data.

Return type:

BootstrapCIResult

ictonyx.bootstrap.bootstrap_paired_difference_ci(group1, group2, n_bootstrap=10000, confidence=0.95, method='bca', random_state=None, return_distribution=False)[source]ÔÉÅ

Bootstrap CI for paired mean differences.

For paired data (e.g. two models evaluated on the same folds), this computes the CI on the mean of the pairwise differences. This is more powerful than the independent two-sample version because it removes between-fold variance.

Parameters:

  • group1 (ndarray | Series | List[float]) ‚Äì Metric values for model 1.

  • group2 (ndarray | Series | List[float]) ‚Äì Metric values for model 2 (same length, paired with group1).

  • n_bootstrap (int) ‚Äì Number of bootstrap resamples (default 10 000).

  • confidence (float) ‚Äì Confidence level (default 0.95).

  • method (str) ‚Äì ‚Äòpercentile‚Äô or ‚Äòbca‚Äô (default ‚Äòbca‚Äô).

  • random_state (int | None) ‚Äì Seed for reproducibility.

  • return_distribution (bool) ‚Äì If True, store the full bootstrap distribution.

Returns:

BootstrapCIResult. The point_estimate is mean(group1 - group2).

Raises:

ValueError ‚Äì If groups differ in length (pairing is impossible).

Return type:

BootstrapCIResult
